key_insight: RZCW-PSS is a sparsity-aware search algorithm that combines coordinate, swap, subspace, and reservoir-based moves to find zero-coordinatewise stationary solutions for NP-hard cardinality-constrained problems.
engineering_relevance: It offers a practical optimization tool for sparse machine learning, with stronger robustness and competitive cost for large nonconvex selection problems.
method: Alternates coordinatewise and symmetry-aware swap support updates
Uses randomized low-dimensional subspace searches from a reservoir of feasible points
Adds sparse-projected reservoir initialization and an optional support-stabilization safeguard
evidence_signal: The abstract reports synthetic sparse learning experiments showing better robustness and solution quality than Partial Simplex Search, Basic Feasible Search, and Zero-Coordinatewise Search, while remaining computationally competitive.
watch_item: The strongest convergence guarantees depend on sampling and subproblem-accuracy assumptions, and the optional safeguard is needed to extend results to all accumulation points.
paper_title: Reservoir Zero-Coordinatewise Projected Subspace Search for Minimization Over Sparse Symmetric Sets in Machine Learning
